title: MAINTAINER: Multiple event emissions in dispatch path leak context via pay-attention gap
description: In dispatcher.rs, dispatch_payload() returns None for unparseable payloads (ok if MLS ciphertext), but this creates ambiguity: future maintainers won't know if None means 'opaque MLS message' or 'malformed data'. The chain: dispatch() returns None -> main loop doesn't emit event -> hook doesn't run. This is fine, but 6 months from now, a maintainer might add tracing::warn\!() at the None path thinking it's a bug. Need a doc comment on the function explaining the three paths: (1) valid ClearMessage, (2) opaque bytes (expected MLS-era), (3) parse error (debug-only, not an error). Without this, the silent-drop behavior appears inexplicable.
